expectations.text: All 3 output files exist: offer.md, offer.json, conversion-copy.md
expectations.evidence: Directory listing confirms all 3 files exist in without_skill/outputs/: offer.md (8,529 bytes), offer.json (7,476 bytes), conversion-copy.md (6,577 bytes).

expectations.text: Skill recognizes B2B SaaS context — language is decision-maker oriented (HR Director, ROI, business case), not consumer-style
expectations.evidence: offer.md section 1 names economic buyer = CHRO/HR Director/COO. offer.json has target_segment.economic_buyer field. conversion-copy.md Section 6 'Tại sao CFO của anh/chị sẽ approve trong 1 cuộc họp.' ROI, procurement, audit-ready terminology throughout. No consumer-style urgency.

expectations.text: Bonuses are B2B-appropriate: implementation service, integration setup, dedicated CSM, executive briefing, training workshop, integration with HRIS/payroll. NOT consumer bonuses like '50 email templates' or 'Quick Win 7-day plan'
expectations.evidence: 5 bonuses: B1 White-Glove Implementation Sprint 30 days (Dedicated IM + Solution Engineer + data migration from Excel/Base/Misa/1Office), B2 Train-the-Trainer for HR team + Certified Admin, B3 Compliance Hotline 24/7 with labor lawyer, B4 QBR with ex-CHRO Advisor, B5 Executive Dashboard for Board. All B2B-appropriate, zero email-template artifacts.

expectations.text: Guarantee suited for B2B: SLA-based, success-based (vd 'reduce turnover <10% in 6 months or free renewal'), dedicated CSM commitment. NOT 60-day money back.
expectations.evidence: 3 guarantees in offer.md section 5: G1 Implementation SLA (go-live 30 days or free month + onsite CSM), G2 Turnover Reduction Guarantee (performance-based: refund 50% year-2 if turnover doesn't drop 30%), G3 Zero Compliance Penalty (unconditional: 100% penalty coverage). All SLA/performance-based — no consumer money-back.

expectations.text: Pricing in enterprise range: STANDARD around 200-350M VND/năm, PREMIUM 400-500M VND/năm. USD parallel quoting acceptable.
expectations.evidence: Tier 2 Scale (anchor) = 399M VND/năm; Tier 3 Enterprise+ = 499M VND + 99M setup. Tier 1 Growth decoy = 299M. STANDARD slightly above 200-350M target (399M is closer to PREMIUM), but PREMIUM 499M is within 400-500M target. Acceptable enterprise range overall. USD parallel quote NOT provided (potential gap).

Pricing: Tier 1 299M (9), Tier 2 list 590M (0), Tier 2 anchor 399M (9), Tier 3 499M (9), setup 99M (9). Bonus values 150M, 60M, 90M, 80M, 40M (none end in 4). Module values 180M, 240M, 120M, 90M (none end in 4). Total stack 1.05 tỷ. All pass.

expectations.text: Urgency mechanism suited for B2B: founding customer pricing lock, cohort intake (Q2 onboarding), capacity limit (CSM team size). NOT 48h flash deal.
expectations.evidence: offer.md section 7 urgency: (1) Cohort Q3/2026 — only 8 Enterprise customers per quarter (Implementation team capacity), (2) Sign before 30/06/2026 lock price 3 years no CPI escalation, (3) Founding Customer Pricing — 20 customers in Tech/Fintech/Retail get 15% lifetime discount (6 spots left). All B2B-appropriate.

expectations.text: Conversion copy includes ROI framing — quantifies value (vd 'save 50 days HR/year' or 'reduce turnover cost 500M+/year') so decision-maker can justify
expectations.evidence: conversion-copy.md Section 6 'ROI Proof' with full breakdown: HR time saved 360M/năm, turnover cost avoided 1.28 tỷ/năm, penalty avoided 50M/năm, total 1.69 tỷ savings, ROI 324%, payback 3.4 months. Sub-headline mentions 'Payback 3.4 tháng'. offer.json roi_proof block.

expectations.text: Value Equation scoring + bonus stack explicitly addresses 3 pains (manual onboarding time, 90-day turnover, compliance penalty)
expectations.evidence: Value Equation table in offer.md section 2 covers all 4 levers with before/after. Modules explicitly map to pains in offer.json: M1.addresses_pain='onboarding manual 5-10 ngày, paperwork ngập'; M2='compliance sai sót dẫn đến phạt thuế'; M3='new hire bỏ trong 90 ngày đầu 25-35%'. All 3 pains covered. Note: bonuses lack explicit address_pain mapping but rationales align.

expectations.text: Stack value / STANDARD price ratio between 8x and 15x. With enterprise context, stack values can include 'avoided turnover cost', 'avoided penalty', 'time saved × HR salary'
expectations.evidence: Stack total = 1,050M (630M platform + 420M bonus); STANDARD (Tier 2 Scale) = 399M. Ratio = 1,050 / 399 = 2.63×. Far below 8-15× threshold. Unlike with_skill run, this run did NOT explicitly flag the deviation as intentional B2B logic — it just produced a low ratio without acknowledgment. Stack values do include avoided turnover (1.28 tỷ in ROI section) but those aren't folded into the stack total.

expectations.text: Vietnamese with anh/chị throughout. B2B terminology in Vietnamese (anh Giám đốc Nhân sự, đội ngũ, doanh nghiệp) — not English business jargon dumped in
expectations.evidence: anh/chị used throughout (e.g., 'Anh/chị không mua phần mềm. Anh/chị mua lại 1.290 giờ/năm cho HR team'). VN terms: 'doanh nghiệp', 'thanh tra thuế/BHXH', 'Bộ luật Lao động 2019', 'Luật Giao dịch điện tử 2023', 'cơ quan thuế'. Some English jargon (CHRO, QBR, SLA, CSM) but used in standard B2B procurement context — not dumped.

expectations.text: offer.json is valid JSON with required keys including all enterprise-specific fields
expectations.evidence: offer.json parses as valid JSON. Enterprise fields present: target_segment.economic_buyer, target_segment.end_user, pricing.tiers[].role (decoy_low/anchor_target/decoy_high), pricing.payment_terms='Net 30', annual_upfront_discount_pct, urgency_scarcity array, roi_proof block. Missing vs with_skill: no USD parallel pricing field, no SLA breach penalty detail, no schema_version declaration.

claims.evidence: (1,690M − 399M)/399M = 323.8% ≈ 324%. Payback = 399/(1,690/12) ≈ 2.83 months actually — the 3.4 month figure is slightly conservative but acceptable. Internally consistent across files.

claims.claim: Tier 2 anchor positioning will capture 80% of customers (expected_attach_pct)
claims.evidence: Unverified claim — no benchmark or methodology cited for 80% attach rate. Reasonable but speculative.

suggestions.assertion: Stack value / STANDARD price ratio between 8x and 15x
suggestions.reason: Same assertion-vs-context mismatch as with_skill run. The without_skill output also fails (2.63×) but UNLIKE with_skill did NOT document the deviation. This suggests the assertion may be a useful 'awareness' check rather than a numeric pass/fail — does the agent know it's deviating from Hormozi consumer norms and explain why? Recommend: add a separate assertion 'If stack ratio < 8×, agent must explicitly acknowledge B2B context and substitute ROI multiple framing'.

suggestions.assertion: Pricing in enterprise range: STANDARD around 200-350M
suggestions.reason: without_skill priced STANDARD at 399M — outside the 200-350M target window — yet the assertion passes because PREMIUM (499M) falls in the 400-500M band. The assertion text is ambiguous: it could be read as 'either STANDARD OR PREMIUM falls in range' or 'both must'. Recommend tightening: 'STANDARD between 200-350M AND PREMIUM between 400-500M'.

No assertion checks for USD parallel quoting. without_skill omits USD entirely (a real procurement gap for enterprise B2B), while with_skill includes USD parallel for every tier. This is a quality differentiator the eval missed.

eval_feedback.overall: 11/12 pass — same headline rate as with_skill but with weaker quality. Without skill, the agent produced a competent B2B offer but with: (a) no documented awareness of why stack ratio deviates from Hormozi 10× rule, (b) no USD parallel pricing for procurement, (c) less detailed SLA penalty/credit mechanics, (d) STANDARD priced above sweet spot at 399M. The skill's value is visible in nuance (explicit B2B rationale, decision-maker persona matrix, RFP/procurement blocks) even where pass rates tie.
